sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: DEFT ALERT: Kirby McInerney LLP Reminds DeFi Technologies Investors of Important Deadline in Class Action Lawsuit
feed.summary: Kirby McInerney LLP is reminding investors of DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) who suffered losses to contact the firm regarding a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors have until January 30, 2026, to apply to be lead plaintiff. The lawsuit alleges that DeFi Technologies made misleading statements about its DeFi arbitrage strategy, understated competition, and was unlikely to meet its revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025, leading to significant stock price declines after negative announcements in November 2025.

feed.title: ProPetro enters power supply agreement with Coterra's unit
feed.summary: ProPetro Holding Corp's energy unit, PROPWR, has signed an agreement with a unit of Coterra Energy to supply power for microgrids in New Mexico's Permian Basin, a project set to begin in Q1 2026. This deal increases ProPetro's 2026 capital spending estimate from $200-$250 million to $250-$275 million due to additional equipment orders, signaling a growing trend for U.S. firms to adopt on-site power generation to reduce fuel costs and enhance reliability.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley's Katerina Simonetti talks her 2026 market outlook
feed.summary: Katerina Simonetti, Executive Vice President at Morgan Stanley Private Wealth, discusses her market outlook for 2026. She provides insights on how investors can construct their portfolios for the upcoming year, offering strategic advice on navigating potential market changes.

feed.title: LTTE: GE Fired me for defending my family — Why that should scare every worker.
feed.summary: A GE Lynn welder, Dan Reynolds, shares his story of being fired by General Electric after defending his transgender sister in a private Facebook group. He argues that his dismissal for an off-the-clock act, unrelated to his work, sets a dangerous precedent for all workers, allowing corporations to police personal opinions and stifle free expression. Reynolds emphasizes that this issue transcends political divides, impacting fairness, freedom, and dignity for every employee.

feed.title: Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) Director Sells $13,910.40 in Stock
feed.summary: Moderna Director Abbas Hussain sold 504 shares of MRNA stock on December 9th for $13,910.40, reducing his position by nearly 25%. This comes as Moderna reported a Q4 EPS of ($0.51) and revenue of $1.02 billion, beating estimates, despite a 45.4% year-over-year revenue decline and negative net margins. Analysts currently have a "Reduce" rating on Moderna with a consensus price target of $29.46.

feed.title: CompoSecure Faces Legal Challenges Over Proxy Statement
feed.summary: CompoSecure (CMPO) is facing legal challenges from shareholders who allege that its Proxy Statement, related to a $3.953 billion deal with Husky Technologies, contains incomplete and misleading information. To avoid further litigation, CompoSecure has chosen to voluntarily supplement the Proxy Statement while denying any wrongdoing. Despite these legal hurdles, the acquisition is seen to provide a positive outlook for future growth, though some financial and valuation concerns remain.

feed.title: Ascendis Pharma Poised for Growth After Price Target Upgrade and Strong Trial Results
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma (ASND) is showing strong potential for growth, with its stock trading up by 4.66% after positive FDA designations and favorable trial results for TransCon CNP. Analysts have upgraded price targets, forecasting significant peak sales for Yorvipath, and the company reported a substantial increase in Q3 revenue. Despite some financial challenges typical of biotech, the market shows bullish momentum and optimism for future regulatory approvals and commercial success.

feed.title: Is Ascendis Pharma’s Surge Sustainable?
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma A/S (NASDAQ: ASND) saw its stock surge by 4.88% due to strong Q3 2025 results and positive pipeline developments for TransCon CNP, despite an FDA PDUFA extension. Analysts from Wolfe Research and Stifel have issued "Outperform" and "Buy" ratings with price targets around $255-$256, underscoring the company’s potential for significant sales growth. However, a notable debt of EUR 365M and negative capital stock and equity indicate financial hurdles that require careful navigation.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Lowers Price Target for General Mills (GIS) to $50
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has maintained its 'Equal-Weight' rating for General Mills (GIS) but lowered its price target from $53 to $50, a 5.66% decrease. This adjustment follows several other analysts lowering their price targets for GIS in recent months, indicating a cautious outlook for the company. Despite the downward adjustments, Wall Street analysts project an average target price of $53.86 for GIS, implying an upside of 15.35% from the current price.

feed.title: Jack Corrigan Buys 5,000 Shares of American Homes 4 Rent (NYSE:AMH) Stock
feed.summary: Director Jack Corrigan recently purchased 5,000 shares of American Homes 4 Rent (NYSE:AMH) for $114,800, increasing his direct holdings by over 40%. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 and reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates. Despite an average "Moderate Buy" rating and a price target of $38.14 from analysts, the stock has experienced recent price target cuts from some firms.

feed.title: Halliburton Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Halliburton Company (NYSE: HAL) will host a conference call on January 21, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. CT to discuss its fourth quarter 2025 financial results. A press release detailing the earnings will be issued prior to the call and made available on Halliburton's website. The call will also be accessible via live webcast, with a recorded version available for seven days afterward.

feed.title: Cooper Companies appoints Brian G. Andrews as principal accounting officer
feed.summary: Cooper Companies announced that Brian G. Andrews, the company's Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, and Treasurer, has taken on the additional role of principal accounting officer. This change, effective Tuesday, was detailed in a recent SEC filing. Andrews assumes these responsibilities from Agostino Ricupati, who will continue with the company as Senior Vice President, Tax.

feed.title: Caterpillar (CAT) Stock After the Bell on Dec. 12, 2025: Why Shares Dropped, After-Hours Move, Analyst Forecasts, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Caterpillar (CAT) stock closed down 4.43% on December 12, 2025, largely due to a broader market risk-off sentiment rather than specific company news. While the company faces ongoing macro uncertainty and tariff pressures, analyst forecasts show a mixed outlook with near-term earnings dips but strong growth projections for 2026. Investors should monitor weekend macro headlines, interest rate expectations, and commodity prices before markets reopen on Monday, December 15, 2025, with the next major catalyst being estimated Q4 earnings on January 29, 2026.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials chief strategy officer Stanley G. Bass to retire in April 2026
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) announced that Stanley G. Bass, its chief strategy officer, will retire on April 30, 2026. This announcement, made via an SEC filing on December 10, indicates that his salary proration and incentive opportunities will remain unchanged until his retirement. The company maintains a strong market position and has reported positive financial results, with its stock trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker VMC.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions approves executive incentive plan for fiscal 2026
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ:SWKS) has announced the approval of its Fiscal Year 2026 Executive Incentive Plan, designed to reward senior management, including named executive officers, based on corporate performance metrics. The plan ties cash incentives to the achievement of annual corporate goals, primarily focusing on revenue and non-GAAP operating income targets. Awards can reach up to 160% of base salary for the CEO and up to 100% for the CFO, with maximum payouts of double the target for exceeding performance.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands CEO Goldstein sells $26.6 million in stock
feed.summary: Robert G. Goldstein, CEO of Las Vegas Sands (NASDAQ: LVS), sold approximately $26.6 million worth of company stock across December 10 and 11, 2025, at prices between $66.39 and $67.03 per share. Concurrently, Goldstein exercised options to acquire 399,900 shares for $20.13 million. Despite the sale, the company shows strong financial health with a "GREAT" score, a P/E of 29.16, and robust gross profit margins of 79.81%, while analysts have raised price targets based on strong performance in Singapore and Macau.

feed.title: Bausch + Lomb allocates $2.8 billion to refinance outstanding loans By Investing.com
feed.summary: Bausch + Lomb Corporation announced it is allocating $2.8 billion in new term B loans to refinance existing debt obligations, aiming to replace outstanding term B loans due in 2031 and 2028. This strategic move is expected to reduce interest margins and maintain the maturity date for some loans while extending others, with the transaction anticipated to close in the first quarter of 2026. The parent company, Bausch Health Companies, maintains a strong financial health score and appears undervalued according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target for EQT to $76 While Maintaining 'Buy' Rating | EQT Stock News
feed.summary: UBS analyst Josh Silverstein has increased the price target for EQT (EQT) to $76 from $67, while reiterating a "Buy" rating for the stock. This adjustment reflects a 13.43% rise in the price target and continued confidence in EQT's market performance. The revised target is higher than the average Wall Street analyst target of $64.39, but the GuruFocus GF Value suggests a potential downside from the current price.

feed.title: 2026 Compliance Guide
feed.summary: Brown & Brown's Regulatory and Legislative Strategy Group has developed the 2026 Employee Benefits Compliance Guide. This guide assists with legislative and regulatory requirements for employee health and welfare benefit plans, covering topics like ACA, ERISA, COBRA, HIPAA, and Medicare requirements.

feed.title: BNY Joins US Govt Investment Initiative, Expanding Access To Savings For Workers
feed.summary: BNY (NYSE: BK) has announced its participation in a U.S. government investment initiative for children, specifically matching the federal government’s $1,000 contribution for eligible newborns of its U.S. employees. This program, part of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act passed by Congress and signed by President Trump, provides a $1,000 pilot contribution into a tax-advantaged account for eligible children born between 2025 and 2028. BNY's involvement aims to double the initial investment, building on its efforts to enhance employees' financial opportunities.

feed.title: Adamas Trust Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Adamas Trust (ADAM) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.23 per share for its common stock, effective for the quarter ending December 31, 2025. Additionally, dividends were announced for several series of its preferred stock. This action reinforces the company's commitment to shareholder returns, making it attractive to income-focused investors, despite a "Neutral" rating from Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, which noted mixed financial performance and high leverage concerns.

feed.title: Join Class Action to Recover Losses from DeFi Technologies (DEFT) - Contact Levi & Korsinsky Before January 30, 2026
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky LLP has filed a class action securities lawsuit against DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) to recover losses for shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between May 12, 2025, and November 14, 2025. The lawsuit claims that the company made false statements and/or concealed issues regarding its DeFi arbitrage strategy, competition, and its ability to meet revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025. Shareholders who suffered losses during this period are encouraged to contact Levi & Korsinsky LLP before January 30, 2026, to learn about their rights to seek recovery.

feed.title: |Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.88
feed.summary: |Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.88 per share, a 4.8% increase from its previous dividend, payable on January 30th to shareholders of record on December 31st. The company has a strong record of 32 consecutive years of dividend increases and a low payout ratio of 22.6%, indicating solid coverage by earnings. Despite recent insider selling totaling approximately $185.4 million, analysts anticipate the medical technology company will post an EPS of $13.47 for the current year.

feed.title: CIBC Maintains Weyerhaeuser Co(WY.US) With Buy Rating, Cuts Target Price to $28
feed.summary: CIBC analyst Hamir Patel has reiterated a "buy" rating for Weyerhaeuser Co (WY.US) but has reduced the target price from $31 to $28. Patel has a 56.2% success rate and an average return of 15.3% over the past year according to TipRanks data. The information is for informational purposes only and not investment advice.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. Declares Fourth Quarter Dividend of $1.56 Per Share
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (MPWR) has declared a fourth-quarter dividend of $1.56 per share for stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025, with payment scheduled for January 15, 2026. The announcement highlights the company's financial stability and commitment to investor returns, despite a safe harbor statement cautioning against over-reliance on forward-looking statements. Insider trading data shows significant sales by executives, while institutional investors show mixed activity in MPWR stock.

feed.title: The Home Depot, Inc. (HD) Under Investigation for Potential Securities Law Violations - Contact Levi & Korsinsky Today
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky has launched an investigation into The Home Depot, Inc. (HD) regarding potential violations of federal securities laws. This follows Home Depot's announced third-quarter fiscal results and updated 2025 guidance on November 18, 2025, which revealed that the company missed management's expectations, causing its stock price to fall over 6%. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm for more information.

feed.title: Alphabet (GOOG) Stock After Hours Dec. 12, 2025: Google News, Analyst Targets, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc.’s Class C shares (NASDAQ: GOOG) saw a volatile Friday, ending lower in regular session trading but stabilizing slightly in after-hours. The stock's movement was influenced by "AI bubble" concerns, new analyst optimism for Google Search, and legal/regulatory headlines from Europe and Russia. Investors are advised to watch weekend news for potential impacts on Monday's market open, particularly regarding regulatory developments and AI narrative shifts.

feed.title: How Amazon is supporting flood-impacted communities in Washington state
feed.summary: Amazon is actively supporting communities affected by historic flooding in Washington state, mobilizing its disaster relief services. The company is prioritizing the safety of its employees and partners, and is delivering essential supplies through its Disaster Relief Hubs in partnership with organizations like the American Red Cross and Save the Children. These efforts include providing hygiene kits, comfort items for children, and medical devices to shelters in western Washington.

feed.title: AMD Stock After Hours (Dec. 12, 2025): Why Shares Fell, What’s Happening After the Bell, and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: AMD stock experienced a significant decline on Friday, December 12, 2025, driven by broader semiconductor and AI sector sentiment rather than AMD-specific news. Concerns about AI profitability and geopolitical factors impacting chip exports influenced investor worries. Despite the day's drop, analyst forecasts remain largely positive, with varying price targets for the stock.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) Stock Price Up 1.3% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) saw its stock price rise by 1.3% after Morgan Stanley increased its price target from $100 to $102, maintaining an "equal-weight" rating. Several other brokers also boosted their targets, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $107.50. The company recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.85 and exceeded quarterly EPS estimates.

feed.title: AI boosts customer service jobs, not cuts: Gartner survey
feed.summary: A Gartner survey reveals that AI is augmenting customer service roles rather than replacing them, with only 20% of organizations reducing headcount due to AI. The study found that 55% of firms maintain stable staffing while handling increased customer volumes, using AI to boost efficiency and optimize workflows. AI is also creating new specialized positions like AI strategists and conversational AI designers, suggesting a catalyst for workforce evolution in customer service and outsourcing.

feed.title: Global payments EVP Sheffield sells $164k in stock
feed.summary: David M Sheffield, EVP and Chief Accounting Officer at Global Payments Inc., sold 2,016 shares of company stock for $164,122. This transaction comes amidst the company's planned acquisition of Worldpay Holdco, LLC and a new partnership with Uber Eats. Several analysts have also raised their price targets for Global Payments, reflecting positive strategic developments.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials chief strategy officer Stanley G. Bass to retire in April 2026
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced that Stanley G. Bass, its chief strategy officer, will retire on April 30, 2026, as per a recent SEC filing. The company is currently valued at $39.15 billion with a 17.21% year-to-date return, and Bass's compensation structure will remain unchanged until his retirement. This announcement follows strong Q3 2025 financial results and positive analyst ratings for the construction materials company.

feed.title: Union Pacific appoints CF Industries CEO Tony Will to board of directors
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation has announced the appointment of W. Anthony "Tony" Will, current President and CEO of CF Industries Holdings, to its Board of Directors, effective January 6, 2026. Will, who is set to retire from CF Industries on January 4, 2026, brings extensive leadership experience to Union Pacific. The railroad company, valued at $142 billion, has a strong financial health rating and a history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: OXY: UBS Maintains Neutral Rating, Lowers Price Target to $43
feed.summary: UBS analyst Josh Silverstein maintained a Neutral rating on Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and lowered the price target from $45 to $43. This adjustment reflects a 4.44% decrease in projected stock value. The consensus among 27 brokerage firms indicates a "Hold" status for OXY, with an average target price of $49.68 from 24 analysts.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Insider Sold Shares Worth $26,567,002, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE: LVS) recently sold shares totalling $26,567,002, as reported in a recent SEC filing. This transaction follows several other insider share sales from the company throughout November and December, alongside various analyst price target adjustments for Las Vegas Sands.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands CEO Goldstein sells $26.6 million in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (NASDAQ:LVS) Chairman and CEO Robert G. Goldstein sold 399,900 shares of common stock for approximately $26.6 million on December 10 and 11, 2025. These sales occurred after Goldstein exercised options for the same number of shares. Following these transactions, Goldstein directly holds 100 shares and indirectly holds 129,005 shares, while the company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score, impressive gross profit margins, and has received positive updates from several financial institutions.

feed.title: This GE Spinoff Is Beating the S&P 500, Nvidia—and Bitcoin. Here’s How.
feed.summary: GE Vernova, a spinoff from General Electric, is outperforming the S&P 500, Nvidia, and Bitcoin by delivering gas turbines at high prices with a substantial backlog. The article highlights how the three-way split of the former General Electric has significantly benefited investors. The author notes that while the success might not have been entirely according to plan, the financial results speak for themselves.

feed.title: Alector (ALEC) Appoints Neil Berkley as CFO
feed.summary: Alector (ALEC) has appointed Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer, effective December 10, 2025, while he will also retain his responsibilities as Chief Business Officer. The biotechnology company, which focuses on neurodegenerative therapies, faces significant financial challenges including negative margins and declining revenue growth. Despite a stable liquidity position, an Altman Z-Score of -3.61 indicates potential bankruptcy risk, and recent insider activity shows substantial selling with no buying.

feed.title: Closing Talent Gaps Requires Addressing Root Causes: 3M
feed.summary: 3M is addressing talent gaps, particularly in STEM and manufacturing, through its "25 Women in Science 2026 – Women in Manufacturing Edition" program. This initiative aims to elevate women in scientific and industrial roles, counteracting early-stage stereotypes and cultural barriers that limit their participation. By creating visible role models and collaborating with various stakeholders, 3M seeks to strengthen the pipeline of female talent and support Mexico's transition towards innovation and advanced industry capabilities.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions approves executive incentive plan for fiscal 2026 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc. (NASDAQ: SWKS) has approved its Fiscal Year 2026 Executive Incentive Plan, which offers cash incentive awards to senior management based on achieving corporate performance metrics like revenue and non-GAAP operating income targets. The plan details potential award percentages of base salary for various executive roles, with the possibility of doubling rewards for exceeding targets. This announcement follows recent financial results where Skyworks surpassed revenue forecasts and met EPS expectations, though its outlook for the mobile segment is mixed.

feed.title: Food Waste Recycling Machines Market and Companies Analysis Report 2019-2031 Featuring BioHiTech Global, Emerson Electric, KCS Engineering, Bhor Engineering, WISErg, IMC Waste Station and More - ResearchAndMarkets.com
feed.summary: The global food waste recycling machine market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 6.6% from 2025 to 2031, driven by increasing food waste, environmental concerns, and government support. Key trends include smart and automated systems, decentralized processing, and waste-to-energy solutions. The market also sees growth in advanced composting, digitalization integration, and diversification of end products, with APAC expected to show the highest regional growth.

feed.title: Union Pacific appoints CF Industries CEO Tony Will to board of directors
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation has announced the appointment of W. Anthony "Tony" Will, CEO of CF Industries Holdings, to its Board of Directors, effective January 6, 2026. Will, who has led CF Industries since 2014 and plans to retire from the company on January 4, 2026, brings extensive leadership experience to Union Pacific. This move is part of the railroad giant's strategy to strengthen its leadership team, following a strong third quarter and significant shareholder approval for a merger with Norfolk Southern.

feed.title: Invitation Homes increases quarterly dividend by 3.4% to $0.30
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (NYSE:INVH) has announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend by 3.4% to $0.30 per share on its common stock, payable by January 16, 2026. This decision reflects the company's consistent dividend growth and comes despite its stock trading near a 52-week low. The announcement follows strong Q3 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations, though Oppenheimer has slightly lowered its price target due to concerns about future rent growth.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ: HBAN) declares $0.35625 Series I preferred dividend
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (Nasdaq: HBANM) has declared a quarterly cash dividend on its 5.70% Series I Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock. The dividend amounts to $356.25 per share, equivalent to $0.35625 per depositary share. It is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2026.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Declares Cash Dividend On Its Series I Preferred Stock
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $356.25 per share ($0.35625 per depositary share) on its 5.70% Series I Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock (Nasdaq: HBANM). This dividend is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2026. Huntington is a regional bank holding company with $223 billion in assets, providing a wide range of financial services.

feed.title: Advisor moves: Raymond James welcomes $692M advisor team in new bank relationship
feed.summary: Raymond James' Financial Institutions Division has partnered with Bank Midwest, integrating the bank's investment program as Midwest Wealth Group and overseeing $692 million in assets. Concurrently, Ameriprise Financial expanded its Kuttin Wealth Management practice by adding four advisors managing nearly $300 million, while Commonwealth Financial Group's Highland Peak Wealth brought on two managing directors from Citi Private Bank and BNY Wealth. These moves highlight the competitive landscape for client assets and top talent in the financial advisory sector.

feed.title: McDonald’s Brings New Scrutiny to Franchisee Pricing in Move to Emphasize Value
feed.summary: McDonald's is implementing new global franchising standards effective January 1, 2026, to ensure franchisees maintain strong value offerings. While franchisees will continue to set their own prices, McDonald's will assess these decisions to reinforce accountability for value leadership across its 44,000+ global restaurants. This move aims to appeal to customers who expect strong value from the brand and follows a period of significant growth for the company.

feed.title: Invitation Homes Raises Quarterly Dividend to $0.30 a Share From $0.29, Payable Jan. 16 to Holders of Record Dec. 23
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.30 per share from the previous $0.29. This updated dividend will be payable on January 16 to shareholders who are on record as of December 23. This news highlights the company's commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems announces $1.56 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:MPWR) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.56 per share, maintaining 12 consecutive years of dividend payments with notable growth. The dividend is payable on January 15, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025. The company, a fabless global provider of semiconductor-based power electronics, has shown strong financial performance with a market capitalization of $45.31 billion and significant stock appreciation year-to-date.
